source: Neural Feature Embedding for User Response Prediction in Real-Time Bidding

concepts.finding: Neural embedding for user response prediction in RTB. Maps high-cardinality categorical features (user ID, ad ID, publisher ID, keyword) to dense embeddings via neural network. Feature embeddings learned end-to-end for CTR prediction. Handles millions of unique feature values.
concepts.implication: Keyword embeddings can be learned from user response prediction tasks (CTR, conversion). Embeddings optimized for prediction naturally capture intent-relevant semantics. Multi-field feature embedding is proven at scale.
concepts.open_question: Do keyword embeddings from CTR prediction capture different semantics than embeddings from co-occurrence? Which is better for intent clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Keyword embeddings learned from CTR prediction tasks produce more intent-discriminative clusters than embeddings from pure co-occurrence (word2vec-style).
